name: Standard Base
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Standard Base recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The 'Egg Yolk' amount is a whole multiple of 18g large egg yolks. The stabilizers are the 4:2:1 'Underbelly General Purpose Stabilizer Blend', total 1.4g. The 15g of 'Invert Sugar' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.

name: French Variation
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's French Variation recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The 'Egg Yolk' amount is a whole multiple of 18g large egg yolks. The stabilizers are the 4:2:1 'Underbelly General Purpose Stabilizer Blend', total 1.05g. The 15g of 'Invert Sugar' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.

name: Light Variation
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Light Variation recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The stabilizers are a 4:3:2 blend, total 1.8g, and 2g of 'Soy Lecithin'. This is inconsistent with 'Underbelly Eggless Ice Cream Stabilizer Blend', which uses a 4:2:1 blend, and 4 'Soy Lecithin'. It's also inconsistent with the 2.1g of stabilizers in the composition table, and with the stated 4.5g of 'Soy Lecithin' in the discussion. The stabilizer blends were both ostensibly edited on 2018-10, but they are inconsistent with each other. The 15g of 'Invert Sugar' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.

name: "Double Origin" Chocolate Ice Cream
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's "Double Origin" Chocolate Ice Cream recipe, posted 2018-09-25, visited 2026-06-16. Source: https://under-belly.org/ice-cream-flavor-chocolate/. Cocoa powder is assumed to be 22% fat, typical of the brands mentioned, e.g. Valrhona, and agrees with the stated total fat content. Chocolate is assumed to be 70% dark chocolate, in the middle of the stated 67-72% range. The stabilizers are a 4:3:2 blend, total 1.8g, and 2g of 'Soy Lecithin' - this does not match any of Underbelly's listed stabilizer blends, though it does match the 'Light Variation' blend. The resulting composition agrees with the stated 'Analysis' - except POD and PAC.

name: Single Origin Cocoa Ice Cream
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Single Origin Cocoa Ice Cream recipe, posted 2018-09-25, visited 2026-06-16. Source: https://under-belly.org/ice-cream-flavor-chocolate/. Cocoa powder is taken as 17% fat, in the middle of the stated 15-20% range. The stabilizers are a 4:3:2 blend, total 1.8g, and 2g of 'Soy Lecithin' - this does not match any of Underbelly's listed stabilizer blends, though it does match the 'Light Variation' blend. The resulting composition agrees with the stated 'Analysis'.

name: Grand Marnier Ice Cream
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Grand Marnier Ice Cream recipe, posted 2016-12-16, visited 2026-06-21. Source: https://under-belly.org/booze-flavored-ice-cream/. The recipe specifies 'Chestnut Honey'. The stabilizers are a unique 5:2:1 blend, total 1.6g

name: Bourbon Smash Ice Cream
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Bourbon Smash Ice Cream recipe, posted 2016-12-16, visited 2026-06-21. Source: https://under-belly.org/booze-flavored-ice-cream/. The stabilizers are the 4:2:1 'Underbelly General Purpose Stabilizer Blend', total 1.4g. 'Lemon Zest' and 'Fresh Spearmint' may only partially contribute to the composition, depending on the preparation, e.g. if the mint is infused rather than blended into the mix. 'Bourbon' is listed as 43%, which Sci-Cream's 'Bourbon Whiskey' matches, but other bourbons can range from 40-45% ABV; one can substitute in 'X% ABV Spirit' to match the specific bourbon used. The recipe states "very fresh mint leaves" and "mint essential oil" without specifying a variety - spearmint is commonly used in cocktails, and peppermint essential oil is most common.
